text.title: Split-Agent Privacy Architecture and Gotchas
text.summary: Policy-classifier + executor split for privacy-safe VUI agents, redaction pipeline, two-channel confirmation, and agent-specific gotchas.

rule.statement: Split every sensitive VUI flow into a policy-classifier agent and an executor agent; the executor never receives raw sensitive slot values.
rule.rationale: A single agent that both classifies sensitivity and generates responses creates a path for PII to appear in "thought", "rationale", or log fields; the split eliminates that path structurally.

rule.statement: Redact sensitive slots pre-storage and pre-LLM-context, not post-hoc.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc redaction fails if the LLM already cached the value or included it in a prior streaming chunk; pre-redaction is the only reliable boundary.

example.note: Policy classifier structured output — no PII echo

antipattern.description: Classifier returns the original utterance text in any field.
antipattern.reason: Any echo of user text in structured output creates a PII logging path; force structured outputs that reference slot names, not values.

rule.statement: For destructive or irreversible voice operations, require confirmation via a second channel (SMS or push notification) — not a follow-up voice prompt.
rule.rationale: Voice spoofing can complete a two-turn voice-only confirmation; a second channel requires device-level possession that a spoofed voice cannot satisfy.

rule.statement: After step-up authentication succeeds, invalidate the cached LLM context; do not allow the "trusted" flag to persist across unrelated turns.
rule.rationale: A persistent auth flag in context enables session hijacking — any subsequent turn in the same session would bypass authentication.

antipattern.description: LLM leaks sensitive slot values into "rationale" or "thought" fields of structured output.
antipattern.reason: Forbid free-text reasoning that quotes user data; force structured outputs that reference slot names only, validated by schema before logging.

antipattern.description: ASR transcripts contain partial sensitive data ("my card is four nine one two...") that regex misses.
antipattern.reason: Combine word-level NER with intent-aware slot deletion; regex alone cannot handle spoken partial values or transposed digits.

antipattern.description: Observability tools (Datadog, Sentry) receive prompt+response containing sensitive data.
antipattern.reason: Configure log scrubbers at the SDK layer before the log sink; agents cannot reliably self-redact in observability calls made by the framework.

antipattern.description: Caching layer re-emits PII because the cache key is the redacted text but the cached value contains the original.
antipattern.reason: Always cache on redacted outputs only; never use unredacted inputs as cache keys with redacted-output values.

text.reference: Regex + slot-map redactor that strips PII from transcripts before LLM or log ingestion.
Structured-output prompt for the vui-privacy-classifier subagent.
